document.diagnosis: Iteration 7 exposed candidate selection and aggregation—not candidate generation alone—as the main bottleneck. Five-base plurality was 0/12 despite a 5/12 candidate oracle, while three-reviewer falsifying selection recovered all five oracle-supported answers. Repair also reached 5/12, but created only one correct answer and fell to 0/4 on the currently weakest sequence family. The nine-answer bank had substantially better coverage—8/12 overall and 3/4 on sequence—but committee compression converted it into only 3/12 exact, including one harmful reversal. The intended six-strategy batch is the three mechanically retained systems plus the first three proposals below; it costs at most 12 × (9 shared base calls + 3 retained-winner reviews + 3 + 1 + 3 proposed reviews) = 228 calls. The fourth proposal is a reserve attribution control.
extensions_requested: sequential_cross_examination: allow later reviewers to see candidate-indexed objections from earlier reviewers and require them to confirm or refute those objections before applying the existing final rule.
most_important_evidence: Five-Bank Falsifying Selection finished 5/12, exactly matching its 5/12 base oracle, with five helpful interventions and no generated repairs.
Five-Bank Falsifying Repair also finished 5/12, but its sole newly created correct answer was in constraint; it scored 0/4 on sequence despite one oracle-supported base candidate there.
Current weakest-family performance was sequence: 1/4 for selection and 0/4 for repair, majority-gated repair, and committee fusion, versus constraint results of 2/4 to 3/4.

The raw nine-answer committee bank contained a correct answer in 8/12 cases and 3/4 sequence cases, compared with 5/12 and 1/4 for the five-answer bank. Committee fusion nevertheless achieved only 3/12 and 0/4 sequence, so hiding raw candidates behind three delegates discarded much of the bank's potential.

Committee fusion made three helpful corrections but also reversed the only base-correct case. The earlier fallback version reportedly made two corrections without a harmful reversal, supporting a direct replication rather than another fusion variation.

Majority gating reduced review calls only from 36 to 30 while dropping exact results from 5/12 to 4/12 relative to always reviewing disagreements; it is not the strongest efficiency direction for the next panel.

proposals.hypothesis: The full nine-answer bank should improve generic candidate coverage while retaining the successful falsifying-selection organization; this tests whether the large oracle gap can be converted without answer-generating repair.
proposals.name: Full-Nine Falsifying Selection

proposals.hypothesis: One falsifying selector may retain a useful fraction of the three-reviewer panel's oracle recovery at six calls per activated case, directly measuring the marginal value of reviewer replication.
proposals.name: One-Review Five-Bank Selection

proposals.hypothesis: Restoring review-plurality fallback without a full-bank delegate vote will test whether the harmful reversal observed in Iteration 7 came from delegate fusion while preserving the orthogonal committee architecture and its positive correction balance.
proposals.name: Committee Comparison Fallback Replication

proposals.hypothesis: A nine-answer deterministic plurality isolates whether any improvement from the larger bank comes from stronger raw consensus rather than reviewer selection.
proposals.name: Nine-Vote Plurality Control

research_hypotheses: Candidate exposure is currently more valuable than unconstrained repair: selection matched the five-bank oracle, whereas repair generated only one additional correct answer and did not improve total exact accuracy.

Increasing the domain-neutral independent bank from five to nine should especially help cases where the smaller bank contains no correct candidate, without introducing family routing or specialized prompts.
If one selector approaches the three-selector result, reviewer replication is a major removable cost; if it collapses, the experiment quantifies the weak-judge failure mode directly.
Committee compression may remain useful for decorrelation, but full-bank delegate fusion is not yet protective against harmful reversals; the earlier fallback architecture needs replication.
The nine-vote plurality reserve separates gains caused by candidate supply from gains caused by review.
document.risk_note: These are 12-case panel estimates with substantial variance. Iteration 7 offered no base-correct cases for the five-bank review systems, so their observed zero harmful reversals do not establish safety. A larger bank raises oracle coverage but may also overwhelm reviewers, a single reviewer may reproduce the known weak-judge problem, and committee delegates can omit a correct raw answer. No result should be called general until it replicates on fresh panels and transfers to untouched exact-verifiable families.
